_format.note: Attachments modify the session in light, lasting ways. Each has a name, voice (the protagonist's reason for carrying it), desc (the mechanical effect), and effect (a handler key consulted in session.js or the run engine). Attachments are carried across the run; the player picks them up via consult nodes and certain quiet events.

resolve.name: Resolve
resolve.voice: I came in with more in me than they wrote down.
resolve.desc: Begin each session with +2 composure (carries above max).

steady.name: Steady
steady.voice: I move slower than the room expects.
steady.desc: Each session has one extra turn before the orderly arrives.

compose.name: Compose
compose.desc: Between sessions, recover an additional 2 composure.

familiar.name: Familiar
familiar.voice: I have read the file before. Of someone.
familiar.desc: At the start of any first encounter, you may see the patient's first telegraphed move TWO turns ahead instead of one.

patient.name: Patient
patient.voice: I am one of them. I have been since admission.
patient.desc: Adjacent damage from being next to a hostile patient is reduced by 1.

remember.name: Remember
remember.voice: Once seen, they do not leave me.
remember.desc: When you enter a familiar patient's room (encountered before), see all their actions in your dossier from the start.

open.name: Open
open.voice: When I let them lead, the room moves with us.
open.desc: Once per session, you may WAIT and the patient stays still that turn as well.

lock.name: Lock
lock.voice: The door I close, I close.
lock.desc: When you carry an item, your composure does not drain from adjacency.
